demo_name: Fred Hutch Governance Session: UC governance on OMOP

deploy_target.note: No bundle to deploy and no clone to run. Governance is Genie-Code-driven policy applied to the SHARED FOUNDATION's 6 OMOP tables, governed IN PLACE. The whole point is to affect the shared data every track reads, so nothing is cloned into isolation, all groups use the same 6 OMOP tables. Set classification and a tag-based policy at the catalog/schema level so it inherits to every child asset the other tracks build. Setup = confirm the foundation's 6 OMOP tables exist, then point Genie Code at the shared foundation schema. The notebooks/, resources/clone_omop_tables.sql, and resources/setup_clone_job.yml are an OPTIONAL facilitator-backup path that clones into a separate schema; the primary path does not clone.

toggle.note: No boolean toggle. Governance operates on ONE schema, the one it governs in place. Synthetic = the shared foundation (client_schema = clinops_foundation, in your own catalog). Real = the FH OMOP schema, a DIFFERENT catalog (curated_omop.omop): point client_catalog/client_schema there and apply the same classification and policy. Table names are identical, so the same masks/filters/tag policy bind either way.

widget_config.note: For the primary govern-in-place path, catalog/schema point at the SHARED FOUNDATION schema being governed (synthetic default clinops_foundation; real curated_omop.omop). At setup the skill rewrites each widget's DEFAULT (2nd arg of dbutils.widgets.text): catalog<-client_catalog, schema<-client_schema, warehouse_id<-warehouse_id, ocdo_group<-ocdo_group. ocdo_group is the account group the masks/row filters gate on via is_account_group_member; the owner/builders see full data, a group member sees the masked/filtered view. Rewriting a widget DEFAULT is a setup action, not a rename, allowed. NOTE: the backup notebooks default schema to clinops_gov (the optional clone path); for the primary path set schema to the foundation.

real_mode.how: Point client_catalog=curated_omop and client_schema=omop, then apply the same classification and tag-based policy to the real OMOP schema in place. The 6 OMOP table names are identical, so the masks, row filters, and tag policy bind unchanged.
real_mode.rebuild_assets: Re-run the classification and policy steps (nb 01-03, or the equivalent Genie Code prompts) against the real schema so tags, masks, and row filters are applied there. Because the policy is set at the catalog/schema level, child assets the other tracks build on the real data inherit it automatically.

tables.name: person
tables.role: Governed in place in the shared foundation. PHI columns: person_id (identifier), year_of_birth, gender_source_value, birth_date. Also carries is_high_profile, a SYNTHETIC-ONLY VIP flag (see the foundation's synthetic_only_columns); it is absent from real curated_omop.omop, so strip references to it in real mode.
tables.access: governed in place (shared foundation schema); masks/filters applied here

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Governed in place. person_id is the row-filter key.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Governed in place. Clinical facts (biomarkers): data_sensitivity, not phi.

tables.name: note
tables.role: Governed in place. note_text is free-text PHI, masked in nb 02; person_id is an identifier.
tables.access: governed in place (shared foundation schema); note_text masked

governed_tag_policy.note: This metastore ENFORCES governed tag policies. You cannot invent tag values. Use only the allowed values from _config's PHI_COLUMNS map.

phi.description: Only accepts HIPAA Safe-Harbor identifier types.

phi_columns.note: The PHI_COLUMNS map in notebooks/_config.py is the source of truth for what gets tagged/masked. Identifiers get phi + data_sensitivity; clinical facts get data_sensitivity only (phi_type is None).

source_files.backup_clone_path: resources/clone_omop_tables.sql + resources/setup_clone_job.yml + databricks.yml, an OPTIONAL facilitator-backup path that clones the 6 OMOP tables into a separate schema. The primary path governs the shared foundation in place and does not use these.

gold.note: The 6 OMOP tables are created by the shared foundation and governed here IN PLACE; the team applies tags/masks/row filters on top. Governance is the foundation every section inherits, the same tag policy set at the catalog/schema level flows down to the DE/ML silver/gold/models built on the shared data.

dependency_map.note: governance does not create the OMOP tables; it governs them in place
nb 02 column mask, gates on is_account_group_member(ocdo_group)

grain_constraints.note: Row-filter key; one row per patient

source_inputs.description: The 6 OMOP tables stood up by the shared foundation, governed here in place. Synthetic default schema = clinops_foundation.
FH real OMOP. Point client_catalog/client_schema here to govern real data in place. Identical table names.

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 1: all 6 OMOP tables present in the governed (shared foundation) schema before applying policy
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl
verify_queries.expected: person=300, condition_occurrence=300, measurement=720, observation=720, drug_exposure=383, note=265

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 2: every PHI column is tagged (coverage check = 0 missing)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT column_name, tag_name, tag_value FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.column_tags WHERE schema_name = '${client_schema}' ORDER BY table_name, column_name
verify_queries.expected: phi + data_sensitivity tags present on the PHI_COLUMNS map columns; 0 missing

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 3: column masks bound to person.person_id, note.person_id, note.note_text
verify_queries.sql: SELECT table_name, column_name, mask_name FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.column_masks WHERE table_schema = '${client_schema}'

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 4: row filter bound across the patient tables
verify_queries.sql: SELECT table_name, filter_name FROM ${client_catalog}.information_schema.row_filters WHERE table_schema = '${client_schema}'
verify_queries.expected: row filter on all 6 tables; owner sees 300 vs the consented subset an ocdo_group member sees

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM table names are standard and the synth->real repointing depends on them staying identical. The 6 tables are owned by the shared foundation and governed here in place; do not rename them.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself

abac_note: Tag-based ABAC via CREATE POLICY (define a policy ONCE by tag and it applies to every column carrying that tag, catalog-wide, including new tables the other tracks build) is the scalable model this session builds toward. Where it is preview-gated on a metastore, the GA column-mask + row-filter path satisfies the full masking/access ask; do not block on ABAC.
govern_in_place_note: CARDINAL PRINCIPLE (inverted from the old clone model on purpose): governance applies classification and policy DIRECTLY to the shared foundation schema, at the catalog/schema level, so every track inherits it and every child asset is covered. This is the whole point, governance must affect the shared data. Masks and row filters gate on is_account_group_member(ocdo_group), so builders/owners keep full access while researchers see the governed view.
checkpoints_note: Checkpoints 1-7 are documented in RUNBOOK.md; nb 05 (AI-feature governance) and nb 06 (inactive users) depend on system tables (serving usage / system.access.audit) that an account admin may need to enable.
